Cipla is injurious to health

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Absolutely nothing. Work culture is too toxic to health and life balance.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
2 years of stress and frustration as a product manager with no learning and growth. Work politics and favorism, Too much urgent and unplanned workload given with only a day's timeline. In a way they don't seem to harass but the only keep you overloaded so that you keep working and have daily sleepless nights. The GPMs don't care about you as there is no guidance and no support. GPM will only brief like a balloon who are themselves blank and clueless of what is to be done. The poor brand managers are always on their own and colleagues don't help you. The GPM will still not help even if you ask but enjoy in the backend of how the product managers are suffering. Not a long term company to work for. It's only a tag that makes your life hell. No work life balance and no family life. Who would want to have family life with no peace?? Everyday ends with stress, anger and frustration both at work and home. Eventhough Saturday's are off, but due to overload and stress, you end up working on Saturdays and Sundays.

Advice
Think before you join. Don't join only for the sake of having a tag on the CV. If you wish to join, need to have a very strong mindset to overcome mental stress, frustrated with family and work, no peace and only overload of work. And they pay you as you demand but we need to think either to have a lot of money or a good family and personal life.
It doesn't matter who you join the next company after Cipla as every organisation model is way different.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Global Presence: Cipla operates in over 80 countries and has a significant global footprint, which allows it to tap into various markets and diversify its revenue streams.

Diverse Product Portfolio: The company offers a wide range of pharmaceutical products across various therapeutic areas, including respiratory, cardiovascular, anti-infective, oncology, and more, catering to diverse medical needs.

Research and Development: Cipla invests significantly in research and development, continuously innovating and developing new drugs and formulations, which helps it stay competitive in the market.

Affordability and Accessibility: Cipla is known for its focus on making healthcare affordable and accessible, especially in developing countries. They often offer generic versions of medications at lower prices, improving access to essential medicines.

Strong Brand Reputation: Over the years, Cipla has built a strong brand reputation for quality and reliability, which has helped it gain the trust of healthcare professionals and consumers globally.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Regulatory Challenges: Operating in multiple countries means facing various regulatory challenges, including different approval processes, compliance requirements, and pricing regulations, which can sometimes pose hurdles to market entry or product launches.

Competition: The pharmaceutical industry is highly competitive, with numerous players vying for market share. Cipla faces competition from both multinational pharmaceutical companies and local generics manufacturers, which can affect its profitability and market position.

Dependency on Patents and Exclusivity: Cipla, like other pharmaceutical companies, depends on patents and exclusivity rights for its products. The expiration of patents or loss of exclusivity for key drugs can lead to increased competition from generic manufacturers and revenue decline.

Supply Chain Risks: As a global company, Cipla is exposed to supply chain risks, including disruptions in the supply of raw materials, regulatory changes impacting manufacturing facilities, and geopolitical tensions affecting distribution networks.

Currency Fluctuations: Operating in multiple countries exposes Cipla to currency fluctuations, which can impact its financial performance, especially when revenues are earned in different currencies than expenses.

Overall, while Cipla has several strengths, it also faces challenges inherent in the pharmaceutical industry. Investors and stakeholders need to consider both the pros and cons when evaluating the company's performance and potential for growth.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Offers competitive pay (that’s it)
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Do not work for this company under any circumstances. You will work for at least 10 hours and on average 12 hours a day. Management will rather demean you and implement verbal and mental abuse/harassment than try to help you improve or create a pathway for you to be successful. It is very understandable for a supervisor to be upset with someone underneath them if they make a stupid mistake, especially if it is repeated. But to be consistently in a bad mood and not be able to have the right communication skills in any case is what this company takes pride in doing.

You will not only be yelled at in public in front of your colleagues but you will have to beg and plead for PTO. You will be told about a ~3% raise every year and be lucky if you get 1%. There is a select group of people who will ALWAYS get a 3% raise and a 10% bonus. But never those who put in work. PPE will be given to you three months (no exaggeration) after you have started doing work.  Waste management is not only poor but negatively affects the workers health. Analysts learn to “deal with it”, but no job is worth shaving off 20 years of your life because the company won’t clean them selves up.

Extremely high turnover rate. When i started 75% of my department was there for less than 6 months. I really hope if someone is still using this website to look for work that they choose not to. I did not do my due diligence before starting and I wish i did. Please save your social life, mental health, and family life and do not work here
